_comment_top: Example retention config for VCF 5.2.x. The default paths assume backups land under /home/backup/b-vcf/ - adjust to your actual SFTP target. Run with --dry-run --verbose first to preview what would be deleted. To temporarily disable a target, set 'enabled': false.

backup_targets._comment: SDDC Manager file-based backup. Files: vcf-backup-<host>-<domain>-<YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM-SS>.tar.gz plus matching .sha256 sidecars. Both extensions are managed together by the preset, so a backup is never left as an orphan checksum file.
backup_targets.name: VCF 5.2.x - SDDC Manager

backup_targets._comment: NSX-T cluster/node backups. Folders: backup-<YYYY-MM-DDTHH_MM_SS>UTC/ under each node UUID directory. Retention is applied PER node automatically.
backup_targets.name: VCF 5.2.x - NSX-T cluster/node backups

backup_targets._comment: NSX-T inventory summaries (small JSON files emitted alongside backups). Pattern: inventory-<YYYY-MM-DDTHH_MM_SS>UTC.json under each node UUID. Per-node retention applies.
backup_targets.name: VCF 5.2.x - NSX-T inventory summaries

backup_targets._comment: vCenter Server file-based backup (VAMI). Two-level structure: <root>/sn_<fqdn>/M_<version>_<YYYYMMDD>-<HHMMSS>_/. The preset matches the inner timestamp folder. vCenter has its own retention setting in VAMI - keep_minimum here is mostly a safety net.
backup_targets.name: VCF 5.2.x - vCenter Server (VAMI)
